steps.assistant_text: 7-insn list-append (count at 0x104, 4-byte entries at 0x108). The long-standing 'sw-before-addu unreachable' cap (documented across 16+ C variants) was actually an as1 SCHEDULER TIE keyed on per-statement debug line numbers. The two independent instructions in question are `addu t8,a0,t7` (compute entry base addr) and `sw t6,0x104(a0)` (store incremented count); the delay slot is `sw a1,0x108(t8)` which depends on t8. With the count-store and entry-store on SEPARATE source lines, IDO's as1 list-scheduler tie-break (line-number ordered) keeps `sw count` first; the target schedules `addu` first so t8 is ready for the delay slot. Collapsing the count-store and entry-store onto a SINGLE source line lets the scheduler interleave them purely by data-readiness, hoisting the addu and matching the target's addu-before-sw order. Whitespace-only steer (semantically identical); the same-line grouping is load-bearing. Same lever as func_00005F34.

metadata.verification: matching-build wordcmp (reloc-masked): 0 non-reloc text diffs, 7==7 insns, same instruction multiset, reloc-set match; build/.o == expected/.o byte-identical; full make ROM byte-identical (cmp clean)
